overview: Expert-level conditionals involve branch prediction optimization, control flow integrity, and writing branching code that is both secure and verifiable. Modern compilers optimize via jump tables and loop unrolling.
industryContext: In high-frequency trading, developers arrange conditions so the CPU's branch predictor guesses correctly most of the time. In safety-critical systems, conditionals must be proven to cover all possible states.

examples.title: Branch Prediction Optimized
examples.explanation: Modern CPUs predict which branch will be taken. If correct, execution continues at full speed. If wrong, the pipeline flushes — a costly operation.

codeReviewChecklist: Does every if have an else?
Is branch order optimized for the common case?
Are there always-true or always-false conditions?

testingPatterns.propertyBasedTests: Generate random inputs to find missed branches

refactoringGuide.smell: Function with 4+ levels of nested if/else
refactoringGuide.solution: Extract into functions, use guard clauses, or replace with polymorphism
steps: Consider a lookup table or strategy pattern

securityConsiderations: Never use eval() with user input
Be careful of timing side channels in security checks
Ensure auth checks run before sensitive branches
Avoid revealing internal state through error messages

scalability.considerations: Large if/else chains become maintenance nightmares. Consider rules engines or decision trees for complex branching.
recommendations: Extract business rules into config files
Log which branches are taken in production

complexityAnalysis.notes: Cyclomatic complexity increases with each branch — keep it under 10 per function
